mission.title: Install and Configure Bpfman on Kubernetes
mission.description: bpfman is an eBPF Manager designed to simplify the deployment and administration of eBPF programs in Kubernetes environments. Installing bpfman allows you to efficiently manage eBPF programs, providing insights and control over their usage in your system.

steps.title: Add the bpfman Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official bpfman Helm repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add bpfman https://bpfman.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install bpfman using Helm
steps.description:
Install bpfman in the 'bpfman' namespace, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install bpfman bpfman/bpfman --namespace bpfman --create-namespace --version v0.5.6
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the bpfman pods are running successfully in the 'bpfman' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace bpfman
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the bpfman deployment to set resource limits for better performance and stability.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment bpfman --namespace bpfman
# Add the following lines under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
```

steps.title: Access the bpfman dashboard
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the bpfman dashboard locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/bpfman --namespace bpfman 8080:80
# Now access the dashboard at http://localhost:8080
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of bpfman will show the bpfman pods running in the 'bpfman' namespace. You can access the bpfman dashboard via port forwarding, allowing you to manage your eBPF programs effectively.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bpfman https://bpfman.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install bpfman bpfman/bpfman --namespace bpfman --create-namespace --version v0.5.6

kubectl edit deployment bpfman --namespace bpfman
# Add the following lines under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"

kubectl port-forward service/bpfman --namespace bpfman 8080:80
# Now access the dashboard at http://localhost:8080

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
